_note: Worked example: Bluestone Mutual Bank, a fictional regional retail/commercial bank. Four source systems (CoreVault Banking, PayStream Hub, CardLink Processor, Sentinel AML) feed a raw->stg->xref->core derived flow, a payment lifecycle state machine, an AML alert funnel, a GL daily summary, a window-sum balance roll-forward, and a transactions-vs-GL reconciliation. Keys starting with '_' are comments stripped before validation.

calendar._note: Card-dominated consumer shape: near-flat week with a Fri/Sat lift (card auths peak Friday-Saturday). The weekend dip on ACH/wire comes from business-day posting logic in the core.posted_transaction derivation, not from the calendar — both channels draw from the same calendar but only payments get posted on business days.

tables.name: application
tables.purpose: Application catalog for the fictional landscape.

tables.name: branch
tables.purpose: Branch network.

columns.name: branch_name
columns._note: city is drawn first so branch names stay coherent with their place.

tables.name: product
tables.purpose: Deposit, CD, and loan product catalog.

columns.name: product_type
columns._note: Drives fk affinity matching from corebank.account.account_type.

tables._note: Customer master. created_at is sorted with 72% backfill so customer IDs correlate with onboarding order and the book is tenured (in-window volume grows ~5%/yr instead of ramping from zero).
tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Core banking customer master (retail, small business, commercial).

columns.name: customer_name
columns._note: Retail customers are persons, businesses get company names — one global generator here is a tell.

tables._note: Accounts open within ~a year of the customer relationship via date_offset from parent.created_at — long activity windows must NOT use date_offset, but onboarding lags are exactly what it is for.
tables.name: account
tables.purpose: Deposit, CD, and loan accounts.

columns.name: product_id
columns._note: Affinity match on product_type with a 2% leak — the leak feeds DQ-001 (account/product type mismatch).

columns.name: activity_tier
columns._note: Volume lever for per_parent scale_by: accounts onboarded late in the horizon get proportionally less activity, so per-parent draws don't dump a full two-year volume into the final weeks (the classic as_of pile-up artifact). parent_copy values arrive ISO-serialized, so plain string comparison buckets the date.

gen.expression: status if customer_since < '2025-04-01' else ('onboarding' if customer_since >= '2025-10-01' else 'ramping')

tables.name: merchant
tables.purpose: Card-accepting merchants seen by the processor.

tables._note: Cards exist mostly on checking accounts (scale_by on account_type); card_status is conditioned on the parent account status so closed accounts carry closed cards.
tables.name: card
tables.purpose: Debit cards issued against deposit accounts.

columns.name: usage_tier
columns._note: Same vintage-tier trick as corebank.account.activity_tier: cards on recently opened accounts spend at a ramp rate, not a two-year rate.

gen.expression: card_status if account_opened_date < '2025-04-01' else ('new_issue' if account_opened_date >= '2025-10-01' else 'ramping')

tables._note: The big card fact. txn_date is a calendar-weighted date bounded by card activation; auth_ts is derived from it via auto_created_at so date and timestamp agree. Volume conditioned on card_status plus a heavy-tailed per-card multiplier (spender whales).
tables.name: card_transaction
tables.purpose: Card authorizations from the processor; approved ones post to accounts.
tables.grain: one row per card authorization attempt

columns.name: merchant_id
columns._note: zipf 1.1: genuinely hot merchants — top 1% of merchants carry ~35-40% of card volume, matching the industry reference.

columns.name: amount
columns._note: price_endings give retail price points (x.99/x.49) — real card amount columns repeat exact values.

tables._note: ACH and wire instructions. Amount realism: lognormal draw quantized by a recurring-payment rounding unit via an expression — recurring payroll/bill amounts repeat exactly (duplicate mass), wires snap to 100/1000. Payment-type mix is conditioned on segment so commercial customers dominate wire value.
tables.name: payment_instruction
tables.purpose: ACH and wire payment instructions flowing through the payment hub.

columns.name: rounding_unit
columns._note: Recurring-payment quantum: 0.01 = exact-cents one-offs; 5/25/100/1000 = recurring payroll, bills, and round wires.

columns.name: wire_scale
columns._note: Wires are commercial-dominated in VALUE: retail wires are small one-offs, commercial wires are large. Applied only to wires in the amount expression so ACH payroll/bill amounts stay segment-neutral.

columns.name: amount
columns.gen: expr:round(round((amount_raw * (wire_scale if is_wire == 1 else 1)) / rounding_unit) * rounding_unit, 2)

tables.name: payment_event
tables.purpose: Payment lifecycle history emitted by the state machine (CDC-style).
tables.grain: one row per instruction per state entered

tables.name: monitoring_rule
tables.purpose: AML transaction monitoring rule catalog.

tables._note: Alerts fire on a small share of payment instructions (~1.5%), skewed toward wires via scale_by — generated next morning by the overnight batch (date_offset +1 day from initiation).
tables.name: alert
tables.purpose: AML monitoring alerts feeding the triage/case/SAR funnel.

tables.name: alert_event
tables.purpose: Alert review history emitted by the state machine.
tables.grain: one row per alert per state entered

tables.name: gl_account
tables.purpose: Chart of accounts: clearing/settlement accounts that tie to transactions plus income/expense accounts.

tables.name: file_load
tables.purpose: Settlement and statement file load log (batch exchange with networks).

tables.name: rule
tables.purpose: Data quality rule catalog (results come from the dq_rule_result_current view).

cases.2: customer missing Sentinel AML party mapping
cases.5: expired identifier mapping still flagged active
cases.6: payment lifecycle events out of sequence
cases.8: product code missing approved GL mapping

tables.name: recon_control
tables.purpose: Reconciliation control register; break details come from the control views.

cases.1: Posted transactions to GL deposits movement
cases.3: Card settlement file to posted card volume
cases.4: AML alert to case linkage completeness

tables._note: Workflow queues. gl_mapping_request cases are raised when a live product code has no approved GL mapping (the asymmetric-mapping coverage gap). queue is conditioned on case_type. case_lifecycle state machine drives status.
tables.name: case
tables.purpose: Exception and review queues (AML mapping requests, recon breaks, KYC).

tables.name: case_event
tables.purpose: Case status history emitted by the state machine.

tables._note: Human-entered mapping table. Finance maintains product code -> GL account hierarchy node in a spreadsheet, uploaded here. Applied asymmetrically: mart_finance_deposits joins it, mart_branchops_deposits uses the raw product code. 12 of 14 live product codes mapped; CHK-STUDENT has only an UNAPPROVED row (stays UNMAPPED); two retired-product rows (effective_end_date in the past); one conflicting SAV-CORE re-upload (last upload wins via row_number).
tables.name: gl_account_mapping
tables.purpose: Finance-maintained product-code to GL-account-hierarchy mapping (spreadsheet upload).

columns.name: source_code
columns._note: Live product codes 1-13 cover CHK/SAV/MMA/CD/loans; 14 is a conflicting SAV-CORE re-upload; 15-16 retired codes.

columns.name: mapped_code
columns._note: GL account hierarchy node: 2010x deposit liabilities, 1500x loan assets.

columns.name: approved_flag
columns._note: Coverage gap (~12/14 live products mapped): CHK-STUDENT has no mapping row at all, and SAV-HY (mapping_id 5) has only this UNAPPROVED draft row -> both fall to UNMAPPED in finance. Retired CHK-LEGACY/SAV-PASSBOOK are approved but effective-ended in the past so they drop out too.

tables._note: Job catalog for the code objects below. job_run history references these; procedures/extracts in catalog.code_object name them.
tables.name: job
tables.purpose: Scheduled job catalog (ETL feeds, EOD/close procedures, refresh proc, outbound extracts).

tables._note: Batch job run log; each run belongs to a catalog job (uniform fk so no skew check fires), with occasional failures. error_note non-null only when failed/partial.
tables.name: job_run
tables.purpose: Batch job run history with occasional failures.

tables._note: Everything below is populated by SQL derivations — real lineage from the source tables above.
tables.name: corebank_customer_extract
tables.purpose: Nightly fixed-width core extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: corebank_customer
tables.purpose: Cleaned core customers; normalizes raw extract dates and casing.

tables._note: Landing layer per feed (rung 1). One raw.* table per source feed beyond the core extract: card transaction file, payment stream events, GL extract. Verbatim text payloads with file/batch ids and ingested_at (clamped <= as_of). format_drift corrupts the date columns post-derivation; the DQ views catch unparseable dates.
tables.name: cardlink_card_txn_extract
tables.purpose: Card processor settlement file as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables.name: paystream_payment_extract
tables.purpose: Payment hub instruction stream as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables._note: GL extract lands from the DERIVED ledger.gl_daily_summary, so its derivation must run after ledger_gl_daily_summary.
tables.name: ledger_gl_extract
tables.purpose: GL daily-movement extract as landed: all text, US-format dates.

tables._note: Staging layer (rung 2). One stg.* per landing table: typed, trimmed, case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL), row_number dedup against re-sent batches.
tables.name: cardlink_card_txn
tables.purpose: Cleaned card transactions; parses landed dates, dedups re-sent batches.

tables.name: paystream_payment
tables.purpose: Cleaned payment instructions; parses landed dates, dedups re-sent batches.

tables.name: ledger_gl
tables.purpose: Cleaned GL daily movement; parses landed dates.

tables.name: entity_identifier
tables.purpose: Identifier crosswalk: CoreVault customer IDs and Sentinel AML party references for canonical customers.

tables.name: customer
tables.purpose: Canonical customer joining staging and the crosswalk.

tables.name: account
tables.purpose: Canonical account enriched with product attributes.

tables._note: The posting ledger: approved card transactions, posted ACH/wire payments (business-day adjusted), and payment returns. The restatement_reversal imperfection lands here post-derivation, creating the transactions-vs-GL recon breaks.
tables.name: posted_transaction
tables.purpose: Canonical posted transactions across card, ACH, and wire channels.
tables.grain: one row per posted transaction, return, or reversal on an account

tables.name: gl_daily_summary
tables.purpose: Daily GL movement by account: double-entry summary derived from posted transactions (deposits GL vs channel clearing GLs).
tables.grain: one row per gl_code per business date with activity

tables.name: case_file
tables.purpose: Investigation cases opened from escalated alerts (every case references an alert by construction).

tables.name: dim_date
tables.purpose: Calendar dimension.

tables.name: dim_customer
tables.purpose: Customer dimension from the canonical layer.

tables.name: dim_account
tables.purpose: Account dimension.

tables.name: dim_merchant
tables.purpose: Merchant dimension.

tables.name: fact_transaction
tables.purpose: Posted transaction fact.

tables._note: Balance roll-forward derived via SQL window sums over the posting ledger — opening + credits - debits = closing holds per account per day by construction, with a deterministic per-account opening anchor.
tables.name: fact_account_balance
tables.purpose: Daily account balance roll-forward.
tables.grain: one row per account per posting date with activity (sparse roll-forward)

tables._note: Materialized view (rung 8). SQLite has no native matviews -> a mart table refreshed by an insert-select from bv_balance_rollforward. Day-grain bank-wide EOD deposit snapshot: ~730 rows (one per posting date with activity) regardless of multiplier, so scale_exempt. Paired with the rebuild_mv_eod_balance_snapshot code object and its job runs.
tables.name: eod_balance_snapshot
tables.purpose: Day-grain bank-wide end-of-day balance snapshot, materialized from bv_balance_rollforward.
tables.grain: one row per posting date with account activity

tables._note: Code-object catalog (rung: catalog). Self-registers every view from sqlite_master, plus authored target-platform source for the EOD posting procedure, GL close procedure, mv refresh procedure, interest-accrual function, and the regulatory/card-network outbound extracts.
tables.name: code_object
tables.purpose: Catalog of deployed code objects (views, procedures, functions, extracts) with source text.

tables.name: lineage_edge
tables.purpose: Lineage edges linking code objects to the tables they read and write.

state_machines._note: Payment lifecycle: initiated -> screened -> posted | returned, with a screening-hold detour and right-censoring so instructions near the as_of date legitimately sit mid-pipeline.
state_machines.name: payment_lifecycle

state_machines._note: AML funnel: ~87% of triaged alerts close as false positives; escalations become cases (derived aml.case_file); ~15% of investigations file a SAR. Both terminal closes stamp the same closed_at column.
state_machines.name: aml_alert_review

state_machines._note: Workflow case lifecycle: open -> triaged -> in_review -> resolved, with an escalation detour. Right-censored so recently opened cases sit mid-flight.
state_machines.name: case_lifecycle

derivations.name: raw_corebank_customer_extract
derivations._note: Raw landing copy with US-format dates; the format_drift imperfection later pads a slice of these, which DQ-003 catches at query time.
sql: select cast(customer_id as text), customer_no, customer_name, segment, kyc_status, city, state,

derivations.name: stg_corebank_customer
sql: select cast(customer_id as integer), customer_no, trim(customer_name), lower(segment), kyc_status, city, state,

derivations.name: raw_cardlink_card_txn_extract
derivations._note: Card processor settlement file landed verbatim as text with US-format dates; raw_card_date_drift corrupts a slice post-derivation (DQ catches it). ingested_at is the next-morning batch, clamped to as_of.
sql: select cast(card_txn_id as text), cast(card_id as text), cast(account_id as text), cast(merchant_id as text),

derivations.name: raw_paystream_payment_extract
derivations._note: Payment hub stream landed verbatim; raw_payment_date_drift corrupts a slice post-derivation (DQ-009).
sql: select cast(instruction_id as text), payment_ref, cast(account_id as text), payment_type, direction,

derivations.name: stg_cardlink_card_txn
derivations._note: Typed/trimmed; case-guarded date parse (drifted formats land NULL); row_number dedup against re-sent settlement batches.
sql: select card_txn_id, card_id, account_id, merchant_id, amount, auth_status, txn_date, ingested_at
select cast(card_txn_id as integer) as card_txn_id, cast(card_id as integer) as card_id,
cast(account_id as integer) as account_id, cast(merchant_id as integer) as merchant_id,
cast(amount as real) as amount, lower(auth_status) as auth_status,
row_number() over (partition by cast(card_txn_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: stg_paystream_payment
sql: select instruction_id, payment_ref, account_id, payment_type, direction, amount, status, initiated_date, ingested_at
select cast(instruction_id as integer) as instruction_id, trim(payment_ref) as payment_ref,
cast(account_id as integer) as account_id, lower(payment_type) as payment_type, lower(direction) as direction,
cast(amount as real) as amount, lower(status) as status,
row_number() over (partition by cast(instruction_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: xref_entity_identifier
derivations._note: Two source systems per canonical customer: the CoreVault customer ID and the Sentinel AML party reference. missing_xref and stale_mapping imperfections degrade this table post-derivation.
sql: select row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,
'CoreVault Banking', 'customer', cast(customer_id as text), date(created_at), null, 1
select 500000 + row_number() over (order by customer_id), 'customer', customer_id,

derivations.name: core_customer
sql: select s.customer_id, s.customer_no, s.customer_name, s.segment, s.kyc_status, s.city, s.state, s.onboarded_date,
where x.canonical_entity_id = s.customer_id and x.source_system = 'Sentinel AML'

derivations.name: core_account
sql: select a.account_id, a.account_no, a.iban, a.customer_id, a.segment, a.account_type,
left join corebank.product p on p.product_id = a.product_id;

derivations.name: core_posted_transaction
derivations._note: Card auths post on transaction date (consumer spend runs 7 days a week); ACH/wire post on the next business day — that derivation step is what creates the weekend dip on human channels while card volume stays flat. Returns reverse the original with direction flipped and the original amount (return amount = original amount invariant).
sql: left join cardlink.merchant m on m.merchant_id = c.merchant_id
case when p.payment_type like 'wire%' then 'wire' else 'ach' end,
case when p.payment_type like 'wire%' then 'wire' else 'ach' end,
case p.direction when 'debit' then 'credit' else 'debit' end,
p.amount, p.counterparty_name, 'returned: insufficient funds or closed account'

derivations.name: ledger_gl_daily_summary
derivations._note: Double-entry by construction: every transaction moves the customer deposits GL (2010) against its channel clearing GL (1310/1320/1330), so total debits = total credits per business date.
sql: select row_number() over (order by business_date, gl_code), business_date, gl_code,
select t.txn_date as business_date, '2010' as gl_code,
case when t.direction = 'debit' then t.amount else 0 end as debit_amount,
case when t.direction = 'credit' then t.amount else 0 end as credit_amount
case t.channel when 'card' then '1310' when 'ach' then '1320' else '1330' end,
case when t.direction = 'credit' then t.amount else 0 end,
case when t.direction = 'debit' then t.amount else 0 end

derivations.name: raw_ledger_gl_extract
derivations._note: GL extract landed verbatim from the DERIVED gl_daily_summary; runs after ledger_gl_daily_summary so the rows exist. raw_gl_date_drift corrupts a slice post-derivation (DQ-010).
sql: select cast(gl_summary_id as text), gl_code, printf('%.2f', total_debit), printf('%.2f', total_credit),

derivations.name: stg_ledger_gl
sql: select gl_summary_id, gl_code, total_debit, total_credit, entry_count, business_date, ingested_at
select cast(gl_summary_id as integer) as gl_summary_id, trim(gl_code) as gl_code,
cast(total_debit as real) as total_debit, cast(total_credit as real) as total_credit,
row_number() over (partition by cast(gl_summary_id as integer) order by ingested_at desc) as rn

derivations.name: aml_case_file
sql: select a.alert_id, 'CASE-' || printf('%05d', a.alert_id), a.alert_id, a.account_id,

derivations.name: wh_dim_date
sql: union all select date(dt, '+1 day') from d where dt < '2025-12-31'
select dt, cast(strftime('%Y', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%m', dt) as integer),
cast(strftime('%d', dt) as integer), cast(strftime('%w', dt) as integer),
case when strftime('%w', dt) in ('0', '6') then 1 else 0 end

derivations.name: wh_dim_customer
sql: select customer_uid, customer_no, customer_name, segment, kyc_status, city, state from core.customer;

derivations.name: wh_dim_account
sql: select account_uid, account_no, account_type, product_code, segment, status from core.account;

derivations.name: wh_dim_merchant
sql: select merchant_id, merchant_ref, merchant_name, mcc, city, state from cardlink.merchant;

derivations.name: wh_fact_transaction
sql: select t.txn_id, t.account_id, a.customer_id, t.txn_date, t.channel, t.txn_type,
join corebank.account a on a.account_id = t.account_id;

derivations.name: wh_fact_account_balance
derivations._note: Window-sum roll-forward: opening + credits - debits = closing per account per posting day, anchored to a deterministic per-account opening balance (no random()).
sql: round(sum(case when direction = 'credit' then amount else 0 end), 2) as credits,
round(sum(case when direction = 'debit' then amount else 0 end), 2) as debits
sum(credits - debits) over (partition by account_id order by txn_date
rows between unbounded preceding and current row) as run_net
select row_number() over (order by account_id, txn_date),

derivations.name: nv_transaction
derivations._note: Normalized view (rung 6): wide re-join of the posted-transaction fact to its dims with business column names. No aggregation. left joins because UNKNOWN MERCHANT / unmastered accounts legitimately lose dim rows. account_key and customer_key share the corebank id space with the dims by construction.
sql: select f.txn_id, f.txn_date, f.channel, f.txn_type, f.direction, f.amount, f.counterparty,
f.account_key, da.account_no, da.account_type, da.product_code, da.segment, da.status as account_status,
left join wh.dim_account da on da.account_key = f.account_key
left join wh.dim_customer dc on dc.customer_key = f.customer_key;

derivations.name: nv_account_balance
derivations._note: Normalized view (rung 6): the balance roll-forward fact re-joined to dim_account. Carries product_code, account_type, segment, and a deterministic is_internal flag (account_id % 50 = 0 are bank-internal settlement/suspense accounts) so the finance-vs-branchops exclusion is exact.
sql: da.account_no, da.account_type, da.product_code, da.segment, da.status as account_status,
case when b.account_key % 50 = 0 then 1 else 0 end as is_internal
left join wh.dim_account da on da.account_key = b.account_key;

derivations.name: nv_gl_posting
derivations._note: Normalized view (rung 6): GL daily movement re-joined to the chart of accounts with readable names and account class. No aggregation beyond the source grain.
sql: select g.gl_summary_id, g.business_date, g.gl_code, ga.gl_name, ga.account_class, ga.is_suspense,
left join ledger.gl_account ga on ga.gl_code = g.gl_code;

derivations.name: bv_balance_rollforward
derivations._note: Business view (rung 7): the roll-forward equation surfaced with a window-lagged opening and a closing recomputed as opening + credits - debits + reversals. Built on nv_account_balance. reversal_adjustment is 0 here (returns already net inside the posting ledger) but the column documents the equation opening + credits - debits + reversals = closing.
sql: select account_key, account_no, account_type, product_code, segment, posting_date,
round(opening_balance + total_credits - total_debits - closing_balance, 2) as rollforward_break,
row_number() over (partition by account_key order by posting_date desc) as recency_rank

derivations.name: bv_deposit_portfolio
derivations._note: Business view (rung 7): segment x product aggregation of each deposit account's latest (as-of) closing balance. Deposit products only (checking/savings/cd/mma); loans carry negative/liability balances and are excluded. recency_rank = 1 picks the latest roll-forward row per account.
sql: select account_key, account_type, product_code, segment, closing_balance, is_internal
from (select account_key, account_type, product_code, segment, closing_balance, is_internal,
row_number() over (partition by account_key order by posting_date desc) as rn
round(sum(case when is_internal = 0 then closing_balance else 0 end), 2) as customer_balance

derivations.name: bv_aml_alert_funnel
derivations._note: Business view (rung 7): the alert -> investigation -> SAR case ladder counted across the lifecycle, with case linkage and override counts. Built on aml.alert + aml.case_file.
sql: case a.status when 'generated' then 1 when 'triaged' then 2
when 'sar_filed' then 7 else 9 end as stage_order
sum(case when c.case_id is not null then 1 else 0 end) as linked_cases,
sum(case when c.sar_filed_date is not null then 1 else 0 end) as sars_filed
left join aml.case_file c on c.alert_id = a.alert_id

derivations.name: mv_eod_balance_snapshot
derivations._note: Materialized view (rung 8): insert-select from bv_balance_rollforward into the mv.eod_balance_snapshot table. Day-grain bank-wide EOD deposit snapshot (one row per posting date), bounded ~730 rows regardless of multiplier. The rebuild_mv_eod_balance_snapshot code object refreshes it on a schedule.

derivations.name: mart_txn_channel_daily
derivations._note: Rewired onto the stack: BU view over the normalized transaction view nv_transaction.
sql: select txn_date, channel, count(*) as txn_count,

derivations.name: mart_aml_funnel
derivations._note: Rewired onto the stack: BU view over the business view bv_aml_alert_funnel.
sql: select funnel_stage as status, alerts, analyst_overrides, avg_risk_score, linked_cases

derivations.name: mart_finance_deposits
derivations._note: Business-unit view (rung 9), FINANCE. GL-centric total deposits: applies the human-entered gl_account_mapping (left join + coalesce 'UNMAPPED', dedup the conflicting SAV-CORE re-upload to the latest approved row via row_number) AND EXCLUDES internal/settlement accounts (is_internal = 1). Competes with mart_branchops_deposits. The mapping is total-preserving (coalesce keeps every balance row), so the only difference vs branchops is the documented internal-account exclusion.
sql: row_number() over (partition by source_code order by uploaded_at desc, mapping_id desc) as rn
where approved_flag = 1 and (effective_end_date is null or effective_end_date > '2025-12-31')
select account_key, product_code, segment, closing_balance, is_internal
from (select account_key, product_code, segment, closing_balance, is_internal,
row_number() over (partition by account_key order by posting_date desc) as rn
from nv_account_balance where account_type in ('checking', 'savings', 'cd')) where rn = 1
left join mapping m on m.source_code = l.product_code and m.rn = 1

derivations.name: mart_branchops_deposits
derivations._note: Business-unit view (rung 9), BRANCH OPS. Account-centric total deposits: raw product_code, NO GL mapping, and INCLUDES internal/settlement accounts. Legitimately disagrees with finance on the grand total by exactly the internal/settlement balance.
sql: select account_key, product_code, segment, closing_balance, is_internal
from (select account_key, product_code, segment, closing_balance, is_internal,
row_number() over (partition by account_key order by posting_date desc) as rn
from nv_account_balance where account_type in ('checking', 'savings', 'cd')) where rn = 1

derivations.name: control_recon_finance_branchops
derivations._note: Reconciles the competing total-deposits metric. branchops_total (includes internal/settlement, raw product code) minus finance_total (internal excluded, GL-mapped) must equal the internal/settlement balance; unexplained_difference proves it (must be 0). finance_unmapped_deposits quantifies the mapping coverage gap (the CHK-STUDENT + SAV-HY UNMAPPED bucket).
sql: row_number() over (partition by account_key order by posting_date desc) as rn
from nv_account_balance where account_type in ('checking', 'savings', 'cd')) where rn = 1
branch as (select round(sum(closing_balance), 2) as total from latest),
fin as (select round(sum(closing_balance), 2) as total from latest where is_internal = 0),
internal as (select coalesce(round(sum(closing_balance), 2), 0) as excluded from latest where is_internal = 1),
and not exists (select 1 from manual.gl_account_mapping m
from branch b, fin f, internal i, unmapped u;

derivations.name: control_recon_txn_vs_gl
derivations._note: Reconciliation as a VIEW so it reflects post-derivation imperfections: the GL summary was built before the restatement_reversal pairs landed in core.posted_transaction, so the affected dates break.
sql: round(t.txn_debits, 2) as txn_debits, round(t.txn_credits, 2) as txn_credits,
round(g.total_debit, 2) as gl_debits, round(g.total_credit, 2) as gl_credits,
sum(case when direction = 'debit' then amount else 0 end) as txn_debits,
sum(case when direction = 'credit' then amount else 0 end) as txn_credits
from core.posted_transaction group by txn_date) t

derivations.name: dq_rule_result_current
sql: select 'DQ-001' as rule_code, 'account product type mismatch' as issue, cast(account_uid as text) as entity_id
from core.account where product_type is not null and account_type <> product_type
select 'DQ-002', 'customer missing Sentinel party mapping', cast(c.customer_id as text)
where not exists (select 1 from xref.entity_identifier x
from raw.corebank_customer_extract where source_created_date not like '__/__/____'
select 'DQ-004', 'card transaction references unknown merchant', cast(c.card_txn_id as text)
and not exists (select 1 from cardlink.merchant m where m.merchant_id = c.merchant_id)
select 'DQ-005', 'expired identifier mapping still flagged active', cast(x.entity_identifier_id as text)
where x.valid_to is not null and x.valid_to < '2025-12-31' and x.active_flag = 1
select 'DQ-006', 'payment lifecycle events out of sequence', cast(e1.instruction_id as text)
on e2.instruction_id = e1.instruction_id and e2.event_seq = e1.event_seq + 1
select 'DQ-007', 'verified customer missing contact email', cast(customer_id as text)
from corebank.customer where kyc_status = 'verified' and (email is null or email = '')
select 'DQ-008', 'product code missing approved GL mapping', p.product_code
and not exists (select 1 from manual.gl_account_mapping m
from raw.paystream_payment_extract where initiated_date_raw not like '__/__/____'
from raw.ledger_gl_extract where business_date_raw not like '__/__/____';

derivations.name: catalog_lineage_edge
derivations._note: Lineage edges for the authored procedures/function/extracts -> the tables they read and write.
sql: insert into catalog.lineage_edge (edge_id, code_object, operation, table_name, edge_note) values

derivations.name: catalog_code_object
derivations._note: Authored target-platform source for the EOD posting procedure, GL close procedure, mv refresh procedure, interest-accrual function, and the regulatory/card-network outbound extracts, then self-registration of every deployed view from sqlite_master so the catalog provably matches the code. Runs LAST so all views exist. deposit_interest_accrual is intentionally stale (references a renamed column) — noted as known debt.
sql: insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes) values

('eod_batch_posting', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure eod_batch_posting(business_date date) language plpgsql as $$ begin insert into core_posted_transaction (account_id, txn_date, channel, txn_type, direction, amount) select c.account_id, c.txn_date, ''card'', ''card_purchase'', ''debit'', c.amount from cardlink_card_transaction c where c.auth_status = ''approved'' and c.txn_date = business_date; insert into core_posted_transaction (account_id, txn_date, channel, txn_type, direction, amount) select p.account_id, date(p.posted_at), case when p.payment_type like ''wire%'' then ''wire'' else ''ach'' end, p.payment_type, p.direction, p.amount from paystream_payment_instruction p where p.posted_at is not null and date(p.posted_at) = business_date; end; $$;', 'core_banking_eng', '2024-01-15 09:00:00', '2025-03-04 11:20:00', 'Nightly end-of-day posting; scheduled by integration.job eod_batch_posting.'),

('gl_period_close', 'procedure', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace procedure gl_period_close(as_of date) language plpgsql as $$ begin insert into ledger_gl_daily_summary (business_date, gl_code, total_debit, total_credit, entry_count) select t.txn_date, ''2010'', sum(case when t.direction=''debit'' then t.amount else 0 end), sum(case when t.direction=''credit'' then t.amount else 0 end), count(*) from core_posted_transaction t where t.txn_date <= as_of group by t.txn_date; insert into workflow_case (case_type, queue, status) select ''recon_break'', ''finance_controls'', ''open'' from control_recon_txn_vs_gl where abs(break_amount) > 0.01; end; $$;', 'finance_controls', '2024-01-20 08:30:00', '2025-02-03 17:45:00', 'Month-end GL close; opens recon-break cases.'),

('rebuild_mv_eod_balance_snapshot', 'procedure', 'sql', 'create or replace procedure rebuild_mv_eod_balance_snapshot() language sql as $$ delete from mv_eod_balance_snapshot; insert into mv_eod_balance_snapshot (posting_date, active_accounts, total_credits, total_debits, total_closing_balance) select posting_date, count(distinct account_key), round(sum(total_credits),2), round(sum(total_debits),2), round(sum(closing_balance),2) from bv_balance_rollforward group by posting_date; $$;', 'data_engineering', '2024-01-18 09:00:00', '2025-01-29 10:10:00', 'Refreshes the EOD balance matview; scheduled daily by integration.job rebuild_mv_eod_balance_snapshot.'),

('deposit_interest_accrual', 'function', 'plpgsql', 'create or replace function deposit_interest_accrual(eod_balance numeric, annual_rate numeric) returns numeric language sql as $$ select round(eod_balance * annual_rate / 365.0, 2); $$;', 'deposits_eng', '2023-10-02 10:00:00', '2023-10-02 10:00:00', 'KNOWN DEBT: an older overload references the retired column account.day_count_basis; flagged for cleanup, still deployed.'),

('regulatory_txn_report_extract', 'extract', 'sql', 'select t.txn_id, t.txn_date, t.account_id, t.channel, t.amount from core_posted_transaction t where t.amount >= 10000 and t.txn_date between date(''now'',''-1 day'') and date(''now''); -- format: fixed-width; destination: sftp://regreporting.example.gov/in/; schedule: 0 6 * * *', 'compliance_eng', '2024-02-10 09:00:00', '2025-05-12 09:15:00', 'Outbound large-transaction regulatory report (job regulatory_txn_report_extract).'),

('card_network_settlement_extract', 'extract', 'sql', 'select c.card_txn_id, c.card_id, c.merchant_id, c.amount, c.txn_date from cardlink_card_transaction c where c.auth_status = ''approved''; -- format: CSV; destination: sftp://network-settle.example.com/out/; schedule: 0 21 * * *', 'integration_eng', '2024-03-01 09:00:00', '2025-04-10 09:15:00', 'Outbound nightly card-network settlement file (job card_network_settlement_extract).');
insert into catalog.code_object (object_name, object_type, language, source_text, owner, created_at, last_altered_at, notes)
from sqlite_master where type = 'view' and name not like 'sqlite_%';

imperfections.name: customer_name_typos
imperfections.scenario: Branch staff hand-key names at onboarding; typos accumulate and feed the MDM match queue.

imperfections.name: dup_customer_onboarding
imperfections.scenario: Same person onboarded twice via branch and mobile channels — split customer view and duplicate AML alerting downstream.

imperfections.name: customer_email_nulls
imperfections.scenario: Contact email never captured for a slice of the book; DQ-007 counts the gap.

imperfections.name: settlement_late_arrival
imperfections.scenario: Card settlement files arrive T+2 instead of T+1 — recon timing noise on daily card volume.

imperfections.name: ghost_merchants
imperfections.scenario: Merchant IDs missing from the processor's merchant master; DQ-004 catches them and the posting ledger shows UNKNOWN MERCHANT.

imperfections.name: payment_event_disorder
imperfections.scenario: Payment hub CDC events land out of sequence; DQ-006 flags status regressions.

imperfections.name: duplicate_file_loads
imperfections.scenario: Scheduler retries log duplicate settlement file loads.

imperfections.name: alert_disposition_overrides
imperfections.scenario: Analysts override model dispositions with supervisor sign-off; visible in mart_aml_funnel.

imperfections.name: missing_party_xref
imperfections.scenario: Mappings lost in the Sentinel AML re-platforming; DQ-002 reports customers without an active party mapping.

imperfections.name: stale_party_xref
imperfections.scenario: Expired identifier mappings never re-pointed after the core conversion; DQ-005 catches active rows with past valid_to.

imperfections.name: raw_extract_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy fixed-width extract job pads dates after a patch; DQ-003 counts unparseable rows.

imperfections.name: raw_payment_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Payment hub stream extract pads dates after a patch; DQ-009 catches the unparseable ones.

imperfections.name: raw_gl_date_drift
imperfections.scenario: Legacy GL extract date format drifts after a patch; DQ-010 catches it.

imperfections.name: gl_restatements
imperfections.scenario: Manual fee reversals posted after the GL summary ran — these create the control_recon_txn_vs_gl breaks.

controls.rule_name: Posted transactions tie to GL deposits movement
Every AML case references an alert
Finance total deposits reconciles to branch-ops total deposits
controls.expected_break_rate: difference equals internal/settlement balance; unexplained_difference must be 0

dq_rules.condition: every customer has an active Sentinel AML party mapping
active mappings have no past valid_to
every live deposit product code has an approved GL mapping

dataflows.name: customer_mastering
dataflows.sequence: corebank.customer -> raw extract -> staging -> xref -> core.customer -> wh.dim_customer

dataflows.name: payment_lifecycle
dataflows.sequence: paystream.payment_instruction -> state machine -> paystream.payment_event -> core.posted_transaction (business-day posting) -> wh.fact_transaction

dataflows.name: balance_rollforward
dataflows.sequence: core.posted_transaction -> window sums -> wh.fact_account_balance -> nv_account_balance -> bv_balance_rollforward -> mv_eod_balance_snapshot

dataflows.name: landing_staging
dataflows.sequence: core/card/payment/GL feeds -> raw.* (verbatim text) -> stg.* (typed, parsed, deduped) -> DQ format rules

dataflows.name: view_stack
dataflows.sequence: wh facts/dims -> nv_transaction/nv_account_balance/nv_gl_posting -> bv_balance_rollforward/bv_deposit_portfolio/bv_aml_alert_funnel -> mv_eod_balance_snapshot / mart_finance_deposits / mart_branchops_deposits / mart_txn_channel_daily / mart_aml_funnel

dataflows.name: deposit_mapping
dataflows.sequence: manual.gl_account_mapping -> mart_finance_deposits (applied + internal excluded) vs mart_branchops_deposits (raw + internal included) -> control_recon_finance_branchops + DQ-008 + workflow gl_mapping_request

dataflows.name: code_catalog
dataflows.sequence: sqlite_master views + authored procs/function/extracts -> catalog.code_object; catalog.lineage_edge; integration.job -> integration.job_run

dataflows.name: aml_funnel
dataflows.sequence: paystream.payment_instruction -> aml.alert -> state machine -> aml.case_file -> SAR filings
